title: Green Thumb Industries Q4 Revenue Hits Record, But One-Time Gain Masks Deep Margin Erosion
text: Green Thumb Industries reported Q4 2025 revenue of $311.1 million, surpassing analyst expectations of $296.05 million with 5.7% year-over-year growth. However, the impressive earnings figures obscure troubling operational deterioration. The company posted diluted earnings per share of $0.35, vastly exceeding the $0.01 consensus estimate, yet this success rests almost entirely on a non-cash gain of $125.9 million from warrant fair value adjustments. Excluding this one-time item, core pre-tax income collapsed to just $2.6 million. The most alarming metric is gross margin's dramatic 830-basis-point plunge to 45.4% from 53.7% year-over-year. This marks the fourth consecutive quarterly decline and reflects intensifying cannabis industry pricing pressures despite the company's strong brand portfolio. Compounding the problem, SG&A expenses climbed to 39.3% of revenue from 34.3% annually, demonstrating dangerous operational deleverage. Retail operations grew 5%, driven predominantly by Minnesota's adult-use market transition. The CPG segment declined 1%, suggesting growth depends more on geographic expansion than core brand strength in established markets. Operating cash flow remained solid at $90 million quarterly.
